perturbation.original_text: DGT shall pay Dolphin USD10,000, for carrying out the beta testing of the quality of their photo-editing services and testing of the Internet bandwidth for file transmission. This amount is to be paid in two monthly installments, USD5,000 on the date of this Agreement and USD5,000, 30 days subsequent to the date of this Agreement
perturbation.changed_text: DGT shall provide Dolphin with a one-time payment of USD10,000 as reimbursement for expenses related to beta testing of the quality of photo-editing services and evaluation of internet bandwidth utilized for file transfers. Payment will be structured in two monthly disbursements: USD5,000 upon signing this Agreement, and the remaining USD5,000, 30 days thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states DGT *shall pay* Dolphin for beta testing, implying a service fee. The modified text rephrases this as DGT *shall provide Dolphin with a one-time payment as reimbursement for expenses*, suggesting a cost recovery rather than a service payment. This introduces ambiguity as to whether Dolphin is providing a service for payment or simply being compensated for costs incurred, which affects interpretations of payment terms, tax liabilities, and potential profit margins. This contradicts section 4, which discusses payment per photo.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Dolphin shall charge DGT USD1.50 per delivered, completed photo. The price per unit shall be open to renegotiation - in response to competitive pricing pressures - upon receipt of written notice at any time by either party;
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the nature of the payment from a fee for service to reimbursement for expenses. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states 'Dolphin shall charge DGT USD1.50 per delivered, completed photo,' indicating a service fee. If the payment is a reimbursement for expenses, then the per-photo charge becomes contradictory and ambiguous, violating the misaligned terminology leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon successful completion of the beta testing stage, DGT will guarantee Dolphin the greater of: (1) USD5,000 for each 30 day period of photo-editing work completed for a total of 90 days (payment to be effective 30 days from the successful completion of the beta tests); or (2) a minimum of 3,300 pictures to be edited for each 30 day period for a total of 90 days at USD1.50 per finished digital photo;
perturbation.changed_text: Following the successful conclusion of the preliminary assessment phase, DGT commits to compensate Dolphin for the higher value derived from either: (1) A fixed sum of USD5,000 for every 30-day interval during which image processing tasks are fulfilled, spanning a cumulative period of 90 days (with payment commencing 30 days after the satisfactory termination of the initial assessments); or (2) The processing of a minimum quantity of 3,300 images within each 30-day cycle over a continuous 90-day duration, priced at USD1.50 for each finalized digital image.
perturbation.explanation: Changed terminology for more clarity, but this creates an in-text contradiction. The original text uses the term *photo-editing work completed*, implying a tangible output required for payment. The modified text changes this to *image processing tasks are fulfilled*, which is vague. It's now unclear if payment is tied to the actual completion of photo editing or merely the performance of processing tasks. This ambiguity affects how Dolphin is compensated and whether quality is a factor in payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Dolphin agrees to complete its photo-editing services within 14 days of receiving the original digital photo files. The completed files must meet DGT pre-defined standards for finished products;
perturbation.justification: The change from 'photo-editing work completed' to 'image processing tasks are fulfilled' introduces ambiguity regarding the criteria for payment. The original text implies payment is tied to finished, quality-assured photo-editing, which aligns with the contradicted text stating 'completed files must meet DGT pre-defined standards'. The modified text, however, could be interpreted as compensating Dolphin simply for performing image processing tasks, regardless of completion or quality, creating a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Dolphin shall maintain at its principal place of business, or such other place as may be most convenient, separate accounts and records of all services provided to DGT, such accounts and records to be in sufficient detail to enable proper returns to be made under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Dolphin is required to keep meticulous financial logs, either at their main commercial site or any other fitting location, chronicling all collaborative endeavors undertaken with DGT. These financial logs must be comprehensive enough to facilitate the creation of precise declarations as mandated by this accord.
perturbation.explanation: By switching from *accounts and records of all services provided to DGT* to *meticulous financial logs chronicling all collaborative endeavors*, it is no longer clear what information needs to be kept. 'Services provided' is a specific category, whereas 'collaborative endeavors' is much broader. The term *collaborative endeavors* can lead to ambiguities and disputes on the scope of required records.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Dolphin shall deliver to DGT on the date 30 days after each and every year a detailed photo-editing report on all services provided on a unit basis (i.e. customer number, file number, cost). Page 2 of 5 The calculation shall be carried out in accordance with generally accepted U.S. accounting principles ("GAAP"), or the standards and principles adopted by the U.S. Financial Accounting Standards Board ("FASB") applied on a consistent basis.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the scope of record-keeping from 'services provided' to 'collaborative endeavors.' The contradicted text requires a 'detailed photo-editing report on all services provided.' By broadening the scope, the change creates ambiguity. It's unclear if 'collaborative endeavors' encompasses only 'services provided' or something more. This ambiguity creates a contradiction because the specific requirement of a 'detailed photo-editing report on all services provided' (Contradicted Text) is now less clear since the record-keeping requirement is related to 'collaborative endeavors' (Changed Text). This misalignment of terminology leads to a strong in-text contradiction, fitting the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
